message:
fix(robustness): improve error handling in info and lock modules

Two related fixes that improve system stability when encountering
unexpected filesystem states.

src/info.ts - Handle corrupt playbook files gracefully:
- Change countPlaybookRules return type from number to number|null
- Return null instead of 0 when playbook parsing fails, distinguishing
  "no rules" from "file is corrupt/unreadable"
- Update formatInfoHuman to display "(invalid/corrupt)" in red when
  ruleCount is null but file exists, instead of misleading "0 rules"
- Users can now diagnose corrupt playbook files from `cm info` output

src/lock.ts - Warn when heartbeat lock disappears:
- When lock heartbeat fails with ENOENT (file not found), log a warning
  indicating the lock may have been stolen or deleted
- Helps diagnose rare race conditions where another process removes a
  lock directory while a heartbeat is active
- Non-ENOENT errors are still silently ignored (best effort heartbeat)

Both changes follow the principle of surfacing actionable diagnostic
information while maintaining fail-safe behavior.
